working: Email open rates are exceptional: the 90-day average open across 100 campaigns is 52.13%, more than double the 25-35% health/nonprofit benchmark, with recent sends like Alumni eNews - June 2026 at 55.31% and Alumni eNews - April 2026 at 53.18%.

Newsletter-driven traffic lifts are real and large: Staff-Client eNews - May 2026 (5/14, 9,731 sends) produced the biggest lift at +76.5% over baseline, followed by Alumni eNews: Final Reminder - June 2026 (6/3) at +65.2% and Alumni eNews - June 2026 (5/20) at +41.2%.
SEO is trending up: GSC impression-weighted average position improved to 17.66 from 19.15 last run, and clicks rose to 25 from 23, while Organic Search holds 35.4% of sessions (813).
Best click engagement came from Alumni eNews - May 2026 (5/2) with a 1.79% click rate and 3.77% click-to-open, well above the in-window average.
Referral traffic edged up to 101 sessions (4.4%) from 96 (4.2%) last run.
not_working: UTM leakage is the dominant problem: all 12 in-window sends show email_sessions_3d = 0 while posting large direct_sessions_3d spikes (Staff-Client May +67 direct, Alumni Final Reminder June +49, Staff-Client March +60). Newsletter links carry no UTM tags, so GA4 misattributes email traffic to Direct, which is inflated to 56.9% / 1,307 sessions.

Several sends had negative lift: Alumni eNews - May 2026 (5/2) at -50.2%, Reminder 2: Alumni eNews - March (3/19) at -33.4%, Movement Reminder Staff-Client - March (3/21) at -30.7%, and Staff-Client eNews - April (4/25) at -15.7%.
Click rates are weak in absolute terms: the 90-day average click is just 2.07%, and most in-window sends land between 0.3% and 1.2% - the strong opens are not converting into clicks.
Organic Social is negligible at 1.7% (38 sessions), below the 5% threshold; Paid Search and AI Assistant are effectively zero at 1 session each.
Total window sessions slipped slightly to 2,295 from 2,309 last run, with Direct (-9) and Organic Search (-11) both down as older high-traffic days roll out of the 90-day window.
do_next: Add utm_source=constantcontact&utm_medium=email (plus a utm_campaign per send) to every newsletter link so GA4 credits Email instead of Direct. This is the single highest-value fix and will finally surface true email-driven traffic now hidden inside the 1,307 Direct sessions.

Repromote the themes that lifted hardest: the large Staff-Client eNews format (May +76.5%, ~9.7k sends) and the Alumni reminder cadence (Final Reminder June +65.2%); retire the May 2 Alumni send format that lost -50.2%.
Close the open-to-click gap: opens are strong (44-55%) but clicks are 0.3-1.2%, so tighten CTAs and subject-to-content alignment on Alumni sends - the problem is engagement, not deliverability.
Send midweek: the three biggest lifts all landed on Wed/Thu (Staff-Client Thu 5/14 +76.5%, Alumni Wed 6/3 +65.2%, Alumni Wed 5/20 +41.2%); keep concentrating sends Tue-Thu.

Sustain SEO momentum: position improved to 17.66, so work the 40 GSC discovery candidates and near-page-1 pages to convert 4,026 impressions into more than 25 clicks, and stand up a light Organic Social cadence (currently 1.7%) to reduce Direct dependence.
summary: All three data sources are live; over the trailing 90 days the site drew 2,295 sessions led by Direct (56.9%) and Organic Search (35.4%), with Staff-Client eNews - May 2026 the top performer at +76.5% lift. The recurring blocker is UTM leakage - every newsletter shows zero attributed email sessions while spiking Direct - so tagging newsletter links is the top priority.
